title: Fleet Repo Metadata (fleet.json)
description: Standard metadata file that every SuperInstance fleet repo must include at its root. Defines repo identity, runtime requirements, test configuration, quality gates, and A2A/ISA conformance.

schema_version.description: Version of the fleet.json schema this file conforms to.

name.description: Repository name (should match GitHub repo name).

description.description: Human-readable description of what this repo does.

owner.description: Primary agent or team responsible for this repo.

health_status.description: Current fleet health status. GREEN=active+tested, YELLOW=stale, RED=placeholder, DEAD=fork.

last_audit_date.description: Date of last fleet-mechanic audit in YYYY-MM-DD format.

ecosystem.description: Fleet ecosystem this repo belongs to (e.g., 'FLUX Core', 'CUDA / cudaclaw', 'Fleet Infrastructure').

runtime.description: Runtime environment requirements. At least one language must be specified.

primary_language.description: Primary programming language of the repo.

rust_edition.description: Rust edition if this is a Rust project.

additional_languages.description: Secondary languages used in this repo.

test_config.description: How tests are run and coverage is measured.

test_command.description: Shell command to run the test suite. Examples: 'pytest', 'cargo test', 'npm test', 'go test ./...'.

coverage_command.description: Shell command to measure code coverage. Examples: 'pytest --cov=src --cov-report=xml', 'cargo tarpaulin --out Xml'.

minimum_coverage.description: Minimum line coverage percentage required to pass the coverage gate.

minimum_test_count.description: Minimum number of tests required to pass the test-count gate.

test_directory.description: Directory where test files are located.

dependencies.description: Dependency declarations, both internal fleet deps and external packages.

repo.description: Name of the fleet repo this depends on.

version.description: Git ref, tag, or commit SHA. Use 'main' for latest.

fleet_internal.description: Internal fleet repos this repo depends on.

workflows.description: Which CI workflows to run. 'fleet-ci' is the standard pipeline.

audit_frequency.description: How often the fleet-mechanic should audit this repo.

nightly_test.description: Run the full test suite nightly in addition to on-push.

require_ci_pass.description: Require all CI checks to pass before merging.

require_review.description: Require at least one review before merging.

quality_gates.description: Quality thresholds that must pass for CI to succeed.

lint_strict.description: If true, linting errors and warnings both cause CI failure.

type_check_strict.description: If true, type checking runs in strict mode.

max_test_duration_seconds.description: Maximum allowed test suite runtime in seconds. CI fails if exceeded.

max_binary_size_kb.description: Maximum compiled binary size in KB. Used for runtime/binary repos.

health_grade_target.description: Target health grade. The fleet-mechanic will flag if the repo drops below this.

isa_version.description: FLUX ISA version this repo targets (e.g., '2.0.0-draft', '1.0.0'). Null if not applicable.

a2a_protocol_version.description: A2A Signal Protocol version supported (e.g., '1.0.0'). Null if not applicable.

i2i_compliant.description: Whether this repo follows Iron-to-Iron communication protocols.

opcode.description: Numeric opcode value (0x00-0xFF) or string identifier.

since_version.description: ISA version this opcode was introduced.

opcodes_implemented.description: FLUX bytecode opcodes this repo's VM implements.

opcodes_used.description: Opcodes that this repo's test programs or tooling depend on.

signal_types.description: A2A signal types this repo can send/receive (e.g., 'ping', 'task_request', 'status_report').

agent_role.description: Role this repo plays in the A2A ecosystem.

health_grade.description: Fleet health grade. A=excellent, B=good, C=needs work, D=at risk.
